comments.en: Ontology for the MDR/IVDR regulatory framework representing Devices, Manufacturers, Incidents, Risks, and Corrective Actions.

comment.en: Risk instance representing device-related thrombosis from riesgos.csv.
Risk of physical harm or tissue damage to the patient.
Field Safety Corrective Action taken to mitigate a risk.
A corporate or administrative entity.
A device intended to be totally introduced into the human body through clinical intervention.
A potential hazard or clinical risk associated with the use of a device.
A formal notification to users regarding device usage instructions.
Risk related to the degradation, wear, or toxicity of the device materials.
An adverse event or serious incident involving a medical device.
A medical device that relies on a source of electrical energy or any source of power.
Risk of the patient developing an infection due to the device.
Instance of an active implantable device from dispositivos.csv.
An active medical device which is intended to be totally or partially introduced into the human body.
A medical device according to the MDR/IVDR regulatory framework.
Instance representing Medtronic plc, extracted from fabricantes.csv.
The corporate entity responsible for manufacturing the medical device.
An IVD device intended by the manufacturer to be able to be used by lay persons.
Risk arising from software vulnerabilities or unauthorized access.
Risk arising from biological or chemical contamination of the device.
Field safety corrective action from acciones_correctivas.csv.
Communication sent to users detailing important safety information.
A medical device intended by the manufacturer for the examination of specimens derived from the human body.
Action taken to correct or update a device without removing it from the user.
Incident report extracted from incidentes.csv.
An incident that directly or indirectly led to death or a serious deterioration in the state of health.
Action to return the medical device to the manufacturer or supplier.
An implantable device that relies on an energy source.
Risk related to false positives or false negatives in IVD devices.
Risk due to electromagnetic or environmental interference.
Risk of the device failing to perform its intended function.
The regulatory authorization number of the manufacturer.
The Unique Device Identifier - Device Identifier.
The number of device units affected by a recall or alert.
The biological sex of the patient.
Boolean flag indicating if the device is implantable.
Connects a corrective action (FSCA) to the incident it addresses.
The commercial name of the medical device.
Boolean flag indicating if the device is active (powered).
The scope of affected products in an FSCA.
The severity level of a risk.
Boolean flag indicating if the device is reusable.
The official EUDAMED reference number for the incident.
The unique identifier for the incident.
The date when the corrective action was initiated.
Links a medical device to its manufacturer.
Links a specific risk category to the types of devices it generally affects.
The likelihood of the risk occurring.
The date when the incident was reported to authorities.
The unique internal identifier for the device.
Links an incident to the identified IMDRF risk category.
Indicates if a corrective action was taken following the incident.
The date when the corrective action was officially closed.
The medical purpose for which the device is intended.
The specific name of the identified risk.
Contact email for the manufacturer.
A textual description of the event, risk, or action.
Indicates the medical device involved in a specific incident.
The unique identifier for the manufacturer.
The name of the entity.
The unique identifier for the risk record.
The unique identifier for the Field Safety Corrective Action.
The official EUDAMED reference number for the FSCA.
List of countries affected by the incident or FSCA.
The age of the patient involved in the incident.
The clinical outcome of the patient following the incident.
The Single Registration Number of the manufacturer in EUDAMED.
The national authority overseeing the manufacturer.
The date when the incident was first detected.
The overarching category of the risk.
Boolean flag indicating if the device is an IVD.
The regulatory risk classification of the device (e.g., Class III).
The country where the incident occurred.
The official IMDRF code identifying the type of risk or event.
The exact date the adverse event took place.
The legal framework applicable to the device (e.g., MDR or IVDR).
The country of origin for the manufacturer.
Identifies the manufacturer that reported the incident to EUDAMED.
The current status of the incident or FSCA (e.g., Open, Closed).
Links a manufacturer to the devices it produces (Inverse of hasManufacturer).
